finding: Homogeneous sets appear more numerous than varied sets because identical items cohere into single perceived whole, increasing apparent area which anchors quantity judgment.

Message source credibility effect reverses when source expertise is incongruent with expressed certainty; low-expertise sources gain persuasiveness by expressing certainty, high-expertise sources by expressing uncertainty.

System threat activation increases defensive behavior equally across confidence levels, but manifests differently: low-confidence individuals choose indirect consumption-based system support while high-confidence individuals choose direct explicit support.
Visual product orientation toward dominant hand facilitates embodied mental simulation and increases purchase intention; blocking perceptual motor resources attenuates this effect.

Product images positioned congruently with spatial-temporal mental representation (past-left, future-right for left-to-right readers) enhance processing fluency and product evaluation; effect reverses for right-to-left readers.

Seller-buyer price discrepancy is larger when products are superior on high-level construal features; discrepancy disappears when products are superior on low-level features, because sellers weight high-level features more heavily than buyers do.
